creator: rwatson@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, 26 Jan 2009, Greg Byshenk wrote:

>> Well, this does raise an interesting question: should we also trim the 
>> setting of IN_ACCESS from ffs_extread() to prevent the access time from 
>> being updated on extended attribute read?  It sounds a bit like the answer 
>> is yes.
>
> Ok, for me it does not seem to solve the problem.
>
> 7.1-STABLE sources as of 20 Jan 2009, patched as directed, complete build of 
> the system, but setting an ACL still changes file mtime.

Indeed, and I can reproduce that here; I was testing with incremental ACL 
updates rather than the initial application which creates the EA.  Sounds like 
I've missed an IN_CHANGE somewhere, presumably somewhere where the block is 
actually allocated, so I'll go do a bit more code reading.  Any thoughts on 
the IN_ACCESS issue?

Robert N M Watson
Computer Laboratory
University of Cambridge
